data.title: Tether’s Bid to Buy Italian Soccer Club Juventus Rejected by Majority Shareholder Exor
data.description:
Tether, the company best known for its popular stablecoin USDT, has made a bold attempt to acquire the renowned Italian soccer club Juventus; however, this proposal has been firmly rejected by Exor, the majority shareholder of the club. Exor, which is controlled by the Agnelli family, holds a significant stake in Juventus and has decided not to entertain Tether’s offer, opting to maintain its current ownership and management structure for the time being. This decision highlights the complexities and competitive nature of sports club ownership in Europe, as well as the challenges facing cryptocurrency companies looking to expand their influence in traditional sectors like professional sports.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/coindesk-news/2025/12/13/tether-s-bid-to-buy-italian-soccer-club-juventus-rejected-by-majority-shareholder-exor

data.title: Wall Street’s Jamie Dimon backs Kevin Warsh for Fed chair
data.description:

data.description:

data.title: Brazil’s Largest Asset Manager Recommends Investors Put Up to 3% of their Money in Bitcoin to Hedge Against FX, Market Shocks
data.description:
Brazil's largest asset management firm has advised investors to consider allocating up to 3% of their investment portfolios to Bitcoin as a strategic move to protect themselves against potential foreign exchange fluctuations and unexpected market shocks. This recommendation reflects a growing trend among financial institutions to recognize the value of digital assets as a means of diversification and risk management in an increasingly volatile economic environment. By investing a modest percentage of their funds in Bitcoin, investors may enhance their overall financial resilience and safeguard their assets against adverse market conditions.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/12/13/brazil-s-largest-asset-manager-recommends-investors-put-up-to-3-of-their-money-in-bitcoin-to-hedge-against-fx-market-shocks
